failure_modes.why: RED built only 2 settlements all game, never expanding past its initial cramped position despite many roads.
RED never upgraded a single settlement to a city, leaving it stuck at 2 base VP plus dev cards.
RED churned countless 3:1/4:1 maritime trades flipping brick/wheat/ore back and forth without converting resources into VP-generating builds.

review_summary: RED stalled at two settlements with no cities and burned its economy on aimless maritime trades, leaving no coherent path to 10 VP while BLUE built cities and stacked dev cards.

failed.claude: Without full board data the node appears to cover limited types, but it's a defensible opening start rather than a clear failure.
failed.openai: Node 25 only provides wheat, leaving RED unable to produce wood, brick, sheep, or ore.

failed.claude: Oracle regret is 0.00 VP-eq, so node 25's pip coverage is effectively equivalent to the best legal option.
failed.openai: Node 25 has only 4 pips, while other nodes like 8 offer higher pip coverage.

failed.claude: State tags note 'boxed_in,' but as the very first opening placement on an open board this is acceptable.
failed.openai: Node 25 is boxed in with limited expansion options.

0.summary: A zero-regret opening settlement on node 25 with solid pip and port value, fully defensible.

28.summary: Converting surplus ore into needed wheat via a 4:1 bank trade is a sound, constructive early move.

70.summary: A reasonable 4:1 ore-for-wheat diversification trade, though buying a development card with the ore surplus would have been slightly more efficient.

122.summary: A solid forced robber move onto a Blue tile that blocks production and steals, matching the oracle's zero-regret evaluation.

162.summary: A reasonable surplus-ore bank trade toward a needed wheat, defensible despite the unfavorable 4:1 ratio.

219.summary: Converting excess ore to wheat to fund a dev-card buy is the optimal, oracle-matching trade.

261.summary: A sensible 4:1 bank trade converting surplus wood into a missing brick from a leading, resource-rich position.

304.summary: Converting surplus brick into the sheep needed to buy a dev card is a defensible, constructive late-game move while far behind and boxed in.

375.summary: A sound robber placement on a productive BLUE tile to steal and slow the leader, matching oracle value.

failed.claude: BUILD_SETTLEMENT 6 was legal and gives an immediate VP plus production, far faster tempo than a road.
failed.openai: Building a settlement at 6 would have provided an immediate VP, which is more urgent than extending the road.

failed.claude: Spending ore on a maritime trade just to build a road yields no VP when settlement 6 would have added a VP directly.
failed.openai: The road build did not directly contribute to VP, whereas a settlement would have.

331.summary: BLUE traded ore for a road to chase longest road while a legal settlement at node 6 (immediate VP) was the clearly better play.

415.summary: Trading 4 surplus sheep for needed wheat is the oracle-optimal, zero-regret move.

460.summary: A defensible 4:1 bank trade of surplus wood into ore from a strong position, only marginally off the oracle's brick preference.

491.summary: Converting excess ore to a needed resource via the port while leading and one VP from victory is a sound, low-risk trade.

560.summary: Sound 4:1 dump of surplus ore for a building resource while one VP from winning; oracle confirms near-zero regret.

591.summary: A reasonable bank trade from a strong, resource-rich position to fuel a dev-card buy while one VP from winning.
